meaning: adj.丧失体面的,可耻的,不名誉的
examples: The defence lawyers claimed that the prisoners had been subjected to cruel and degrading treatment.
Mr. Porter was subjected to a degrading strip search.
Extending life grows medically feasible, but it is often a life deprived of everything, and one exposed to degrading neglect as resources grow over-stretched and politics turn mean.
The new TSA screening techniques are especially outrageous, but their screening techniques have always been degrading.
But the market is still over-saturated with female characters in degrading or objectified roles.
